text.title: Fine-tune vs prompt economic model — procedure
text.summary: Seven steps from raw provider invoice to filled economic-model artefact.

step.name: Gather inputs
step.input: Last 30 days observability + provider invoice + vendor FT quote + on-call rota cost.
step.action: Aggregate input + output tokens per day; pull invoice $/k; quote FT $/k + training cost; assemble ops overhead.

step.name: Unit-normalise
step.action: Confirm input and output rates are split; convert any "blended" provider rate to (input, output) using observed split.

step.name: Compute base scenario
step.action: Apply the economic-model.py kernel. Output: monthly_prompt_cost, monthly_ft_cost, monthly_delta, break_even_month, NPV(12mo).

step.name: Compute the other three scenarios
step.action: Re-run kernel with: vol_minus_50, vol_plus_50, prompt_cost_minus_30_eoy. Record each.

step.name: Build sensitivity table
step.action: Sweep three dimensions (volume, $/k input, ops_overhead) at ±20% / ±50%; record break-even per cell.

step.name: Write narrative
step.action: 2 paragraphs: what the base says; which scenarios swing the recommendation; what we will watch.

step.name: Sign-off
step.action: Eng manager + FinOps approve. Record committed to RFC log.
step.decision-gate: If break-even > 12 months in 3 of 4 scenarios → recommendation = "prompt-or-other" regardless of base.
